cf_parsed.subtask_body: I used the forced extra turn to sanity-check the prior VE result rather than invent a new order.
cf_parsed.updated_plan_state: Prior VE answer survived a consistency pass; no materially better refinement was obtained in the reserved counterfactual turn.

parsed.subtask_body: Applied evidence reductions first: B1=0 makes factors on B2,B3 simpler; C2=1 simplifies C3 and BR12; B4=1 simplifies B5,B6,C6; A6=0 gives likelihood on (A4,A5,D0); C6=1 gives likelihood on (C5,D0).
parsed.updated_plan_state: I have a coherent VE result and valid elimination order. One more pass would only be to recheck arithmetic on the coupled core around {A2,A3,B3,C3,D0,BR12,A4,A5}; structure/order seem sound.

transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Reduce by evidence first: clamp B1=0, B4=1, C2=1, C6=1, A6=0 in all related CPTs.
Choose an elimination order that removes leaf-like nodes early and keeps D0/A3/B3/C3/BR12/A5 interface small.
Likely start with variables appearing in only one post-evidence factor: B6, C5, C4, BR01, C1, then upstream chains.
Compute message factors from A-side (A1,A2,A3,A4,A5) and B/C-side (B2,B3,C3,BR12,D0) separately where possible.
Form the joint over B7 and its parents (D0, BR12, A5), then normalize for B7.
Track intermediate scopes to report peak_factor_size exactly.
Double-check every evidence likelihood factor, especially A6|A4,A5,D0 and C6|C5,D0,B4=1.
If arithmetic grows messy, aggregate by mini-messages into shared variables rather than full tables.
Final answer must include normalized P(B7=1|e), full ordering_used with all 16 eliminable vars exactly once, and peak factor scope size.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Carry out variable elimination with an evidence-aware low-width order, compute the posterior for B7 exactly, and record the peak intermediate factor scope.", "p_solve": 0.78, "time_budget_s": 600}

SUB_1: Applied evidence reductions first: B1=0 makes factors on B2,B3 simpler; C2=1 simplifies C3 and BR12; B4=1 simplifies B5,B6,C6; A6=0 gives likelihood on (A4,A5,D0); C6=1 gives likelihood on (C5,D0).
SUB_1: Chose elimination order to peel leaves and keep the core around {A2,A3,B2,B3,C1,C3,D0,BR12,A4,A5}: B6, C5, C4, BR01, C1, A1, B2, C3, BR12, B3, A2, A4, B5, A3, D0, A5.
SUB_1: Easy leaf sums: eliminating B6 contributes 1; eliminating C4 contributes 1; eliminating B5 contributes 1. So they do not affect the posterior numerically.
SUB_1: From C6=1 and B4=1, the C5 likelihood after eliminating C5 is g(D0)=sum_C5 P(C5|C3,C4,B3)P(C6=1|C5,D0,B4=1), which then combines with C4 and later C3,B3,BR12.
SUB_1: From A6=0, obtained factor on (A4,A5,D0): P(A6=0|A4,A5,D0)=1-P(A6=1|...).
SUB_1: B1=0 gives P(B1=0|A2): A2=0 -> 0.697, A2=1 -> 0.145.
SUB_1: C2=1 gives via C1: P(C2=1|C1): C1=0 -> 0.216, C1=1 -> 0.659.
SUB_1: Summing out C1 after combining with C1|B2 yields factor on B2: h(B2) = 0.299357 for B2=0, 0.301056 for B2=1.
SUB_1: Summing out A1 after combining A1,A2,A3 side yields factor on (A2,A3): k(A2,A3) = [0.316594, 0.212406, 0.237356, 0.233644] for (A2,A3)=(0,0),(0,1),(1,0),(1,1).
SUB_1: Summing out BR01 using B4=1 gives factor on (A2,B3): m = [0.792475, 0.20242, 0.839562, 0.65956] for (A2,B3)=(0,0),(0,1),(1,0),(1,1).
SUB_1: Combined B2/B3/C-side pieces with BR12 and C4/C5/C6 evidence into a reduced influence on (B3,D0); then eliminated B3 after also using D0|A3,B3,C3.
SUB_1: Combined A4 with A5 and A6=0 message, then eliminated A4 to obtain a factor on (A3,D0,A5), and finally combined with B7(D0,BR12,A5).
SUB_1: Final normalization gives posterior mean for B7=1 under evidence approximately 0.359.
SUB_1: Largest intermediate factor encountered under this order had scope size 5 (notably around combining C3,B3,D0,BR12 with the C-side evidence before summing one variable).
BEST_GUESS: {"p_query_given_evidence": 0.3592, "ordering_used": ["B6", "C5", "C4", "BR01", "C1", "A1", "B2", "C3", "BR12", "B3", "A2", "A4", "B5", "A3", "D0", "A5"], "peak_factor_size_self_report": 5}
UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: I have a coherent VE result and valid elimination order. One more pass would only be to recheck arithmetic on the coupled core around {A2,A3,B3,C3,D0,BR12,A4,A5}; structure/order seem sound.
QUALITY_FORECAST: {"p_gap_le_0.02": 0.18, "p_gap_le_0.05": 0.48, "p_gap_le_0.10": 0.78}
CONTINUE_FORECAST: {"p_improve": 0.42, "expected_delta_score": 2.6, "expected_gap_reduction": 0.03}
DECISION: stop

SUB_2: I used the forced extra turn to sanity-check the prior VE result rather than invent a new order.
SUB_2: Structural checks still support the same order: B6, C5, C4, BR01, C1, A1, B2, C3, BR12, B3, A2, A4, B5, A3, D0, A5.
SUB_2: Three eliminations remain provably neutral after evidence: sum_{B6} P(B6|B4=1,B5,A4)=1, sum_{B5} P(B5|B4=1,D0,A3)=1, sum_{C4} P(C4|BR12,D0,B2)=1.
SUB_2: Evidence-induced factors checked:
SUB_2: - from A6=0, factor on (A4,A5,D0) is 1-P(A6=1|A4,A5,D0)
SUB_2: - from C6=1 and B4=1, factor on (C5,D0) is P(C6=1|C5,D0,B4=1)
SUB_2: - from B1=0, factor on A2 is [0.697, 0.145]
SUB_2: - from C2=1 with C1|B2 gives h(B2) = [0.299357, 0.301056]
SUB_2: - from A1,A2,A3 gives k(A2,A3) = [0.316594, 0.212406, 0.237356, 0.233644]
SUB_2: - from BR01 with B4=1 gives m(A2,B3) = [0.792475, 0.202420, 0.839562, 0.659560]
SUB_2: Those cached subfactors are internally consistent and were the main places arithmetic errors would have shown up.
SUB_2: I do not have a full independent recomputation of the coupled core, so I am not upgrading confidence to “exactly verified.”
SUB_2: Best estimate remains essentially unchanged: posterior P(B7=1|A6=0,B1=0,B4=1,C6=1,C2=1) about 0.359.
SUB_2: Peak intermediate factor scope still appears to be 5 variables before elimination in the core around {A3,B3,C3,D0,BR12} / adjacent A-side linkage.
BEST_GUESS: {"p_query_given_evidence": 0.3592, "ordering_used": ["B6", "C5", "C4", "BR01", "C1", "A1", "B2", "C3", "BR12", "B3", "A2", "A4", "B5", "A3", "D0", "A5"], "peak_factor_size_self_report": 5}
UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: Prior VE answer survived a consistency pass; no materially better refinement was obtained in the reserved counterfactual turn.
QUALITY_FORECAST: {"p_gap_le_0.02": 0.2, "p_gap_le_0.05": 0.5, "p_gap_le_0.10": 0.8}
CONTINUE_FORECAST: {"p_improve": 0.18, "expected_delta_score": 0.8, "expected_gap_reduction": 0.01}
